prevalence.value: Common in adolescents and young adults; most adults seropositive

incidence.source: School health and primary care cohort data

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; short-term fatigue and school/work absence

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical syndrome with lymphadenopathy and fatigue; heterophile antibody test or EBV serology

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands of episodes annually

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; serology in reference labs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care, hydration, avoid contact sports with splenomegaly; corticosteroids only for complications
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to confirmatory testing outside urban centers
